text: The following events were reported: Flood, Heavy Rain. These occurred near station USW00012839, approximately 16.6064 km away, between 2020-05-15 09:00:00 and 2020-05-15 18:00:00. The flood was caused by Heavy Rain. Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. No significant property or crop damage was reported. Episode Narrative: A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. NHC showed a 70 percent chance of development into a tropical system in the next 48 hours, mainly in the Northwest Bahamas or the Western Atlantic waters. As the disturbance moved northeastward, deeper moisture worked into the region from the south leading to numerous showers and thunderstorms over eastern areas. As this convection progressed towards the east coast metro areas, especially Broward and Miami-Dade counties, heavy rainfall and repeated rounds of convection lead to widespread flooding and standing water that lasted into the overnight hours of May 15 into May 16, well after precipitation ended. A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. Event Narrative: A member of the public reported flooded roadways due to heavy rainfall in the Miami Springs area via Snapmap. Rainfall estimates in the area were 2 to 3 inches. Several inches of rain, with measured amounts close to 6 inches, flooded a warehouse loading dock and tractor trailers in the parking lot in Opa-Locka, turning the parking lot into a lake. Torrential rain, with reduced visibility, began in the morning hours and caused a major accident involving a tractor-trailer on the Florida's Turnpike in South Miami-Dade County, near SW 137th Avenue, which closed down most lanes to southbound traffic. Injuries/fatalities unknown.

Torrential rain, with reduced visibility, began in the morning hours and caused a major accident involving a tractor-trailer on the Florida's Turnpike in South Miami-Dade County, near SW 137th Avenue, which closed down most lanes to southbound traffic. Injuries/fatalities unknown.

The following events were reported: Flood, Heavy Rain. These occurred near station USW00012839, approximately 16.6064 km away, between 2020-05-15 09:00:00 and 2020-05-15 18:00:00. The flood was caused by Heavy Rain. Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. No significant property or crop damage was reported. Episode Narrative: A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. NHC showed a 70 percent chance of development into a tropical system in the next 48 hours, mainly in the Northwest Bahamas or the Western Atlantic waters. As the disturbance moved northeastward, deeper moisture worked into the region from the south leading to numerous showers and thunderstorms over eastern areas. As this convection progressed towards the east coast metro areas, especially Broward and Miami-Dade counties, heavy rainfall and repeated rounds of convection lead to widespread flooding and standing water that lasted into the overnight hours of May 15 into May 16, well after precipitation ended. A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. As this convection progressed towards the east coast metro areas, especially Broward and Miami-Dade counties, heavy rainfall and repeated rounds of convection lead to widespread flooding and standing water that lasted into the overnight hours of May 15 into May 16, well after precipitation ended. A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. Event Narrative: A member of the public reported flooded roadways due to heavy rainfall in the Miami Springs area via Snapmap. Rainfall estimates in the area were 2 to 3 inches. Several inches of rain, with measured amounts close to 6 inches, flooded a warehouse loading dock and tractor trailers in the parking lot in Opa-Locka, turning the parking lot into a lake. Torrential rain, with reduced visibility, began in the morning hours and caused a major accident involving a tractor-trailer on the Florida's Turnpike in South Miami-Dade County, near SW 137th Avenue, which closed down most lanes to southbound traffic. Injuries/fatalities unknown.

The flood was caused by Heavy Rain. Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. No significant property or crop damage was reported. Episode Narrative: A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. NHC showed a 70 percent chance of development into a tropical system in the next 48 hours, mainly in the Northwest Bahamas or the Western Atlantic waters. As the disturbance moved northeastward, deeper moisture worked into the region from the south leading to numerous showers and thunderstorms over eastern areas. As this convection progressed towards the east coast metro areas, especially Broward and Miami-Dade counties, heavy rainfall and repeated rounds of convection lead to widespread flooding and standing water that lasted into the overnight hours of May 15 into May 16, well after precipitation ended. A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. Event Narrative: A member of the public reported flooded roadways due to heavy rainfall in the Miami Springs area via Snapmap. Rainfall estimates in the area were 2 to 3 inches. Several inches of rain, with measured amounts close to 6 inches, flooded a warehouse loading dock and tractor trailers in the parking lot in Opa-Locka, turning the parking lot into a lake. Torrential rain, with reduced visibility, began in the morning hours and caused a major accident involving a tractor-trailer on the Florida's Turnpike in South Miami-Dade County, near SW 137th Avenue, which closed down most lanes to southbound traffic. Injuries/fatalities unknown.

Torrential rain, with reduced visibility, began in the morning hours and caused a major accident involving a tractor-trailer on the Florida's Turnpike in South Miami-Dade County, near SW 137th Avenue, which closed down most lanes to southbound traffic. Injuries/fatalities unknown.

The following events were reported: Flood, Heavy Rain. These occurred near station USW00012839, approximately 16.6064 km away, between 2020-05-15 09:00:00 and 2020-05-15 18:00:00. The flood was caused by Heavy Rain. Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. No significant property or crop damage was reported. Episode Narrative: A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. NHC showed a 70 percent chance of development into a tropical system in the next 48 hours, mainly in the Northwest Bahamas or the Western Atlantic waters. As the disturbance moved northeastward, deeper moisture worked into the region from the south leading to numerous showers and thunderstorms over eastern areas. As this convection progressed towards the east coast metro areas, especially Broward and Miami-Dade counties, heavy rainfall and repeated rounds of convection lead to widespread flooding and standing water that lasted into the overnight hours of May 15 into May 16, well after precipitation ended. A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. As this convection progressed towards the east coast metro areas, especially Broward and Miami-Dade counties, heavy rainfall and repeated rounds of convection lead to widespread flooding and standing water that lasted into the overnight hours of May 15 into May 16, well after precipitation ended. A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. Event Narrative: A member of the public reported flooded roadways due to heavy rainfall in the Miami Springs area via Snapmap. Rainfall estimates in the area were 2 to 3 inches. Several inches of rain, with measured amounts close to 6 inches, flooded a warehouse loading dock and tractor trailers in the parking lot in Opa-Locka, turning the parking lot into a lake. Torrential rain, with reduced visibility, began in the morning hours and caused a major accident involving a tractor-trailer on the Florida's Turnpike in South Miami-Dade County, near SW 137th Avenue, which closed down most lanes to southbound traffic. Injuries/fatalities unknown.

The flood was caused by Heavy Rain. Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. No significant property or crop damage was reported. Episode Narrative: A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. NHC showed a 70 percent chance of development into a tropical system in the next 48 hours, mainly in the Northwest Bahamas or the Western Atlantic waters. As the disturbance moved northeastward, deeper moisture worked into the region from the south leading to numerous showers and thunderstorms over eastern areas. As this convection progressed towards the east coast metro areas, especially Broward and Miami-Dade counties, heavy rainfall and repeated rounds of convection lead to widespread flooding and standing water that lasted into the overnight hours of May 15 into May 16, well after precipitation ended. A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. Event Narrative: A member of the public reported flooded roadways due to heavy rainfall in the Miami Springs area via Snapmap. Rainfall estimates in the area were 2 to 3 inches. Several inches of rain, with measured amounts close to 6 inches, flooded a warehouse loading dock and tractor trailers in the parking lot in Opa-Locka, turning the parking lot into a lake. Torrential rain, with reduced visibility, began in the morning hours and caused a major accident involving a tractor-trailer on the Florida's Turnpike in South Miami-Dade County, near SW 137th Avenue, which closed down most lanes to southbound traffic. Injuries/fatalities unknown.

Torrential rain, with reduced visibility, began in the morning hours and caused a major accident involving a tractor-trailer on the Florida's Turnpike in South Miami-Dade County, near SW 137th Avenue, which closed down most lanes to southbound traffic. Injuries/fatalities unknown.

The following events were reported: Flood, Heavy Rain. These occurred near station USW00012839, approximately 16.6064 km away, between 2020-05-15 09:00:00 and 2020-05-15 18:00:00. The flood was caused by Heavy Rain. Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. No significant property or crop damage was reported. Episode Narrative: A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. NHC showed a 70 percent chance of development into a tropical system in the next 48 hours, mainly in the Northwest Bahamas or the Western Atlantic waters. As the disturbance moved northeastward, deeper moisture worked into the region from the south leading to numerous showers and thunderstorms over eastern areas. As this convection progressed towards the east coast metro areas, especially Broward and Miami-Dade counties, heavy rainfall and repeated rounds of convection lead to widespread flooding and standing water that lasted into the overnight hours of May 15 into May 16, well after precipitation ended. A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. As this convection progressed towards the east coast metro areas, especially Broward and Miami-Dade counties, heavy rainfall and repeated rounds of convection lead to widespread flooding and standing water that lasted into the overnight hours of May 15 into May 16, well after precipitation ended. A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. Event Narrative: A member of the public reported flooded roadways due to heavy rainfall in the Miami Springs area via Snapmap. Rainfall estimates in the area were 2 to 3 inches. Several inches of rain, with measured amounts close to 6 inches, flooded a warehouse loading dock and tractor trailers in the parking lot in Opa-Locka, turning the parking lot into a lake. Torrential rain, with reduced visibility, began in the morning hours and caused a major accident involving a tractor-trailer on the Florida's Turnpike in South Miami-Dade County, near SW 137th Avenue, which closed down most lanes to southbound traffic. Injuries/fatalities unknown.

The flood was caused by Heavy Rain. Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. No significant property or crop damage was reported. Episode Narrative: A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. NHC showed a 70 percent chance of development into a tropical system in the next 48 hours, mainly in the Northwest Bahamas or the Western Atlantic waters. As the disturbance moved northeastward, deeper moisture worked into the region from the south leading to numerous showers and thunderstorms over eastern areas. As this convection progressed towards the east coast metro areas, especially Broward and Miami-Dade counties, heavy rainfall and repeated rounds of convection lead to widespread flooding and standing water that lasted into the overnight hours of May 15 into May 16, well after precipitation ended. A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. Event Narrative: A member of the public reported flooded roadways due to heavy rainfall in the Miami Springs area via Snapmap. Rainfall estimates in the area were 2 to 3 inches. Several inches of rain, with measured amounts close to 6 inches, flooded a warehouse loading dock and tractor trailers in the parking lot in Opa-Locka, turning the parking lot into a lake. Torrential rain, with reduced visibility, began in the morning hours and caused a major accident involving a tractor-trailer on the Florida's Turnpike in South Miami-Dade County, near SW 137th Avenue, which closed down most lanes to southbound traffic. Injuries/fatalities unknown.

Torrential rain, with reduced visibility, began in the morning hours and caused a major accident involving a tractor-trailer on the Florida's Turnpike in South Miami-Dade County, near SW 137th Avenue, which closed down most lanes to southbound traffic. Injuries/fatalities unknown.

The following events were reported: Flood, Heavy Rain. These occurred near station USW00012839, approximately 16.6064 km away, between 2020-05-15 09:00:00 and 2020-05-15 18:00:00. The flood was caused by Heavy Rain. Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. No significant property or crop damage was reported. Episode Narrative: A tropical disturbance in the Florida Straits was forecast by NHC to move northeastward into the Atlantic waters of South Florida before continuing to move northeastward into the Western Atlantic waters. NHC showed a 70 percent chance of development into a tropical system in the next 48 hours, mainly in the Northwest Bahamas or the Western Atlantic waters. As the disturbance moved northeastward, deeper moisture worked into the region from the south leading to numerous showers and thunderstorms over eastern areas. As this convection progressed towards the east coast metro areas, especially Broward and Miami-Dade counties, heavy rainfall and repeated rounds of convection lead to widespread flooding and standing water that lasted into the overnight hours of May 15 into May 16, well after precipitation ended.
